title: PPI Network Overview (vis-network)
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Bioinformatics
description: Interactive force-directed protein-protein interaction network with 25 cancer-related proteins, hub highlighting, and network statistics using vis-network

learningObjectives: Identify hub proteins in a PPI network by visual size and color cues
Interpret network statistics including degree, density, and clustering coefficient
Explain the biological significance of highly connected proteins in signaling pathways
Compare betweenness centrality across nodes and explain its role in network communication
Recognize modular structure in biological networks through interactive exploration
prerequisites: Basic graph theory concepts (nodes, edges, degree)
Familiarity with protein names and cell signaling pathways

keyQuestions: Which protein has the highest degree and why might that matter biologically?
What does a high betweenness centrality suggest about a protein's role in cellular communication?
Can you identify clusters of proteins that interact more with each other than with the rest of the network?
How does the network structure reflect known cancer signaling pathways?
commonMisconceptions: Hub proteins are always the most important drug targets — in reality, targeting hubs can cause widespread side effects
All edges in a PPI network represent direct physical binding — some may represent functional associations
A high clustering coefficient means a protein is a hub — it actually measures local neighborhood connectivity
assessmentOpportunities: Ask students to identify the top 3 hub proteins and explain their biological roles
Have students predict what would happen to the network if TP53 were removed
Students compare clustering coefficients of hub vs non-hub proteins and explain the difference
